name: TextField
summary: Single- or multi-line text input with label/helperText/error support; controlled via `value` + `onChange` or auto-bound to a `FormControl` by `name` — the default for free-form text entry.

bestPractices: Always provide a clear `label`. Avoid placeholder‑only fields; labels remain visible and accessible.
Choose the correct `type` (e.g., `email`, `password`, `number`) and set `autoComplete` to improve UX and a11y.

Pick a control model deliberately: use `value` for app‑state control; use `defaultValue` for simple local inputs. Resolution precedence is explicit `value` prop > FormControl binding > internal state, latched at mount — passing both a `value` prop and a form `name` dev‑warns (the prop wins for rendering; edits still write through to the store).

In forms, set a stable `name` so values submit predictably, and seed every bound key in `createFormStore(initial)`. An unseeded `name` renders `defaultValue ?? ''` as a controlled value with a one‑time dev warning and never writes to the store on mount, so it stays absent from submit/reset until the first edit.
Use `as='textarea'` with `rows` for multi‑line input; keep width comfortable and avoid forcing long single‑line fields.

Prefer the canonical `onValueChange`/`onValueCommit` events over raw `onChange`: each `ChangeInfo.source` is classified honestly — typing reports `keyboard`, paste/drop reports `clipboard`, programmatic value writes report `programmatic`; Enter commits as `keyboard` and blur commits as `programmatic`.
Convey issues via `error` + `helperText`. Keep helper text succinct; reserve errors for actionable feedback.
Use `fullWidth` in narrow or linear layouts; otherwise size with `sx` or parent layout for consistency.
Prefer theme tokens and `preset` over ad‑hoc CSS to keep density and colours consistent.

examples.title: Email + Password
examples.code:
<Stack gap={1}>
  <TextField name='email' type='email' label='Email' placeholder='you@example.com' autoComplete='email' />
  <TextField name='password' type='password' label='Password' autoComplete='new-password' />
</Stack>

examples.title: Multiline with helper
examples.code:
<Stack gap={1}>
  <TextField name='bio' as='textarea' rows={4} label='Bio' placeholder='Tell us about yourself' helperText='Use 2–3 sentences.' />
</Stack>
